title: Hugging Face's Clem Delangue on Open Source AI and the LLM Bubble | MTS Live
content: Clément Delangue, CEO of Hugging Face, discusses the global open‑source AI race, the large‑language‑model (LLM) bubble—he argues it lies in API‑based models—and envisions robotics becoming the next major AI interface. He addresses AI safety, U.S.–China competition, the rise of open‑weight models, and how Hugging Face serves as the infrastructure backbone for open‑AI development.

title: 让智能体看见世界：CV × AI Agent 的行业场景新实践| 2026AI Partner·北京亦庄AI+产业大会
content: The talk explains how integrating computer vision (CV) with AI agents creates a multiplier effect, enabling digital agents to understand and act on the physical world. Daqia Inc. has built the Xinghan model series—V for vision, L for language, M for multimodal—to give systems a clear perception of global and local environments. These models power industry‑specific agents that learn business logic and perform autonomous tasks, as demonstrated in public safety, industrial inspection, and forest fire prevention. The goal is to embed AI as a secure, industry‑aligned “outside brain” rather than just a chatbot.

title: 投了OpenAI 130亿的微软，被自家工程师用Claude Code烧爆了账本
content: Microsoft will shut down all use of Anthropic’s Claude Code by the end of June, moving its ≈100,000 engineers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI. The decision is driven by rising token‑based costs, as each line of code generated by Claude costs Microsoft money. Similarly, Uber’s CTO reported the company had exhausted its 2026 AI budget in just four months, highlighting how token‑pricing is turning AI from a productivity tool into a significant budget drain.

title: 教室带宽不低于100兆、学生座椅有靠背，《中小学基本办学条件底线要求》发布 | 原文
content: The Education Ministry and three other departments released the “Basic School Operating Conditions – Bottom‑Line Requirements,” setting new standards for primary and secondary schools. The guidelines cover five areas: school buildings, safety, living facilities, teaching equipment, and staff. They include 20 minimum requirements, such as a minimum 100 Mbps classroom internet bandwidth, student chairs with backs, flagpoles, safety upgrades for dangerous houses, campus security systems, and fire safety compliance. The goal is to meet all standards across all schools within three years.
eventKey: 教室带宽不低于100兆-学生座椅有靠背-中小学基本办学条件底线要求

title: AI That Empowers Every Dream: My Vision Inspired by Google I/O 2026
content: Google I/O 2026 showcased AI tools that became truly student‑friendly, enabling beginners to build projects quickly with reduced infrastructure complexity. The author is motivated by the vision “AI That Empowers Every Dream,” aiming to create practical, impactful applications such as a smart study assistant, health and wellness aid, traffic solutions, and rural education tools in local languages. The event highlighted Google AI Studio’s streamlined workflows, encouraging independent developers. The author calls for more guided learning paths to support rapid, confidence‑boosted AI experimentation.

title: As a Gen AI Student, Here’s What Excited Me Most at Google I/O 2026
content: I, a B.Tech student focused on Generative AI, was most excited by the unveiling of Google AI Studio and the Gemini ecosystem at Google I/O 2026. The platform promises rapid prototyping, easy integration, and beginner‑friendly workflows, lowering barriers for students, indie developers, and startups. My own projects—an AI‑powered Smart Kitchen and a traffic‑optimization tool—illustrated how accessible tools can turn everyday ideas into practical solutions. The event underscored AI’s growing role in routine development and its wider, democratized impact.

title: Anthropic 将向 SpaceX 支付近 450 亿美元以获取算力
content: Anthropic PBC will pay SpaceX nearly $45 billion over three years for computing resources to support its Claude AI software. SpaceX disclosed that Anthropic will pay about $1.25 billion monthly until May 2029, with either party able to terminate the agreement with 90 days’ notice.
